cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the best title for this passage. The passage is about body language in the office and what not to do. It starts by saying body language is powerful and that 50% of what we mean is communicated through body movements, while words are only 7%. Then it lists several things to avoid in the office: not touching co-workers, avoiding eye contact, having a bad handshake, and leaning the head while listening. The question is asking which of the four options is the best title. The options are A, B, C, and D. Let me break down each one. Option A: "Pay attention to your body language at the office." The passage is definitely about body language in the office context, and it's giving tips on what not to do. So this seems relevant. Option B: "How to get along with your co-workers." While the passage does mention co-workers, the focus is specifically on body language, not general ways to get along. So maybe B is too broad. Option C: "The meaning of body language you should know." The passage does explain some meanings of body language, but it's specifically about what not to do in an office setting. This option might be too general and not specific to the office. Option D: "Different countries have different body language." The passage doesn't mention anything about different countries, so D can be ruled out. So between A, B, and C. The passage is focused on the office environment and what mistakes to avoid. A is about paying attention to body language at
